probe: int4 QAT W4A16 greedy run-to-run determinism characterization (diagnostic only)
purpose: Answer advisor 11:23 question: under which config is int4 M=1 greedy run-to-run reproducible; isolate cudagraph vs eager, batch composition, sampler flag. Reconcile DIVERGENT (PR#3) with reported within-stack IDENTICAL.

comparisons.name: eager M=1 run1 vs run2
comparisons.note: from prior PR#3 artifacts; first divergences early (idx 9-418)

comparisons.name: cudagraph M=1 run1 vs run2
comparisons.note: REAL submission config; divergences all LATE; some single-token (idx462 -> 8 div tokens)

comparisons.name: concurrent (co-sched) run1 vs run2
comparisons.note: MOST run-to-run stable; 7/8 byte-identical; identical batch composition -> most consistent reduction order

comparisons.name: concurrent vs M=1 (composition effect)
comparisons.note: SAME prompt -> DIFFERENT greedy tokens under co-scheduling vs M=1; confirms split-K/batched-reduction-order sensitivity (advisor hypothesis)

flashinfer_sampler_flag: irrelevant to greedy: temperature=0 takes hard argmax and bypasses the sampler; run-to-run noise lives in forward kernels (Marlin split-K GEMM, Triton-attn reductions), not the sampler. VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=0 is only a local nvcc/curand workaround.
conclusions: No config gives guaranteed bit-exact run-to-run identity at output_len 512: a rare near-tie logit can always FP-flip the argmax and cascade.
But greedy IS reproducible per-sequence when no near-tie is hit (most prompts are byte-identical run-to-run). It is a near-tie-incidence continuum, not a binary.
Divergence severity is config-ordered: eager M=1 (worst) > cudagraph M=1 > concurrent fixed-batch (best run-to-run).
Batch composition is a token-changing variable: co-scheduling vs M=1 changes the greedy tokens of the SAME prompt (confirms split-K reduction-order sensitivity).

Therefore the int4 base cannot self-certify the hard greedy gate against a SEPARATELY-generated reference; it passes only via the official within-run/same-job reference (consistent with the VALID leader and the advisor Q1 answer).

Reconciles with a within-stack IDENTICAL control: 7/8 of my prompts are perfectly reproducible; a control whose prompts/length/comparison-method hits zero near-ties returns 8/8 IDENTICAL - same mechanism, different near-tie incidence.
suggested_followup: Isolate prefix-cache / scheduler state as the run-to-run driver by re-running cudagraph M=1 back-to-back with --no-enable-prefix-caching (one restart). Not run here to keep the probe within the advisor's named toggles.
